info.description:
REST API for OpenVibely - AI-powered task scheduling and management
This API provides endpoints for managing projects, tasks, and chat interactions with AI agents.
info.title: OpenVibely API

get.description: Returns model usage, success count, and failure count grouped by project.
Returns average execution durations grouped by configured model.
get.summary: Get average execution time by model

get.description: Returns average task execution durations for completed executions.
get.summary: Get average execution time by task

parameters.description: Maximum number of tasks to return
parameters.name: limit

get.description: Returns execution counts grouped by hour-of-day.
Returns tasks with repeated failures, grouped by last observed error.

parameters.description: Maximum number of patterns to return
parameters.name: limit

get.description: Returns tasks ordered by execution count.

parameters.description: Maximum number of tasks to return
parameters.name: limit

get.description: Returns execution success/failure rates grouped by day, week, or month.

parameters.description: Grouping period: day, week, or month
parameters.name: group_by

get.description: Returns build-chain status HTML for an autonomous build root task.

put.description: Updates autonomous build settings for a project and returns an updated HTML dashboard fragment.

parameters.description: JSON array of protected file paths
parameters.name: protected_files

get.description: Returns the build summary panel HTML for an autonomous build root task.

post.description: Runs trend pattern analysis and returns analysis result HTML.
Collects trend data from configured sources and returns collection result HTML.
Runs competitor update analysis and returns result HTML.

get.description: Returns the trend dashboard HTML section for a project.

patch.description: Updates a trend pattern status and returns updated patterns HTML.

parameters.description: Feature name when status is implemented
parameters.name: feature_name

post.description: Adds a monitored trend source and returns updated source-list HTML.

delete.description: Deletes a trend source and returns updated source-list HTML.

patch.description: Enables or disables a trend source and returns updated source-list HTML.

post.description: Starts a new autonomous build chain for a project and returns an HTML fragment with result status.

put.description: Saves X API credentials for trend intelligence and returns an HTML confirmation fragment.

get.description: Returns global worker pool usage and available slots.
Returns worker capacity information for models that have explicit model-level limits.
Returns worker capacity information for a specific model configuration.
Returns worker capacity and queue information for each project.
Returns worker capacity and queue information for a specific project.

post.description:
Send a chat message to the AI agent with optional file attachments.
Returns 201 immediately with a message ID. The AI processes the message asynchronously.
Poll GET /api/chat/message/{id} to check status and retrieve the response.
Supported file types: Images (JPG, PNG, GIF, WebP), Documents (PDF, TXT, MD, CSV), Code (Go, Python, JS, TS, Rust, Java, C/C++, Ruby, PHP, Swift, Kotlin, Shell, SQL, HTML, CSS, SCSS, XML, JSON, YAML, TOML, INI, diff/patch)
Maximum file size: 10 MB per file
Maximum files per request: 10
post.summary: Send a chat message with optional file attachments (async)

parameters.description: Chat message text to send to the AI
parameters.name: message

parameters.description: Project ID to associate with this chat message
parameters.name: project_id

parameters.description: File attachments (screenshots, images, PDFs, text files)
parameters.name: attachments

400.description: Bad request - missing required fields or invalid input

get.description:
Poll this endpoint to check if an async chat message has completed processing.
Returns the current status (processing, completed, failed) and the response when available.
get.summary: Get the status and result of a chat message

post.description: Performs AI-powered semantic analysis of what files, APIs, schemas, and components a task will likely modify

patch.description: Updates the status of a conflict prediction (acknowledge, resolve, mark as false positive)

get.description: Returns all active conflicts involving a specific task

post.description: Compares impact analyses across pending/running tasks to predict conflicts

get.description: Returns historical conflict data for a project (for learning)

post.description: Records a conflict that actually occurred for learning and prediction improvement

get.description: Returns the latest impact analysis for a task

post.description: Generates an optimal task execution order recommendation based on detected conflicts

get.description: Returns the latest pending recommendation for a project
Returns a full collision report for a project including analyses, conflicts, and recommendations
Get a list of all available projects with their IDs, names, paths, and creation timestamps
Returns the best-performing model for the requested task type based on workflow metrics.
get.summary: Get best model for task type

200.description: Best model and metric payload, or message when unavailable

get.description: Returns the cheapest model that meets the requested minimum quality threshold.
get.summary: Get cheapest model for task type

200.description: Cheapest model and metric payload, or message when unavailable

get.description: Returns workflow performance metrics for all configured model configurations.
Returns workflow performance metrics for a specific model configuration.
Returns all agent vote records for a workflow voting step execution.
get.summary: Get vote records for workflow step execution

task_ids.description: JSON array of task IDs in order
